p-1.plaintext: Add data attributes to your rendered HTML to enable progressively richer visual editing:

children.text: Add data attributes to your rendered HTML to enable progressively richer visual editing:

ul-2.plaintext: **`data-block-uid="blockId"`** — Click-to-select blocks. Hydra.js adds click handlers and shows a blue outline and Quanta toolbar on selected blocks. **`data-edit-text="fieldName"`** — Inline text editing. For simple text, click and type directly. For rich text (slate widget), select text to apply formatting via the Quanta toolbar. **`data-edit-media="fieldName"`** — Visual media uploading. Editors can upload, pick or drag-and-drop images directly onto the element. **`data-edit-link="fieldName"`** — Link editing. Click behaviour is replaced with a link picker to select content, enter an external URL, or open the link.

children.text: **`data-block-uid="blockId"`** — Click-to-select blocks. Hydra.js adds click handlers and shows a blue outline and Quanta toolbar on selected blocks.
**`data-edit-text="fieldName"`** — Inline text editing. For simple text, click and type directly. For rich text (slate widget), select text to apply formatting via the Quanta toolbar.
**`data-edit-media="fieldName"`** — Visual media uploading. Editors can upload, pick or drag-and-drop images directly onto the element.
**`data-edit-link="fieldName"`** — Link editing. Click behaviour is replaced with a link picker to select content, enter an external URL, or open the link.

children.text: If you can't modify the markup (e.g., using a 3rd party component library), use comment syntax to specify block attributes:

Title

Read more

ul-8.plaintext: Attributes without selectors apply to the root element: `block-uid=xxx` Attributes with selectors target child elements: `edit-text=title(.card-title)` Closing `<!-- /hydra -->` marks end of scope Self-closing `<!-- hydra block-uid=xxx /-->` applies only to next sibling element

children.text: Attributes without selectors apply to the root element: `block-uid=xxx`
Attributes with selectors target child elements: `edit-text=title(.card-title)`
Closing `<!-- /hydra -->` marks end of scope
Self-closing `<!-- hydra block-uid=xxx /-->` applies only to next sibling element
Supported attributes: `block-uid`, `block-readonly`, `edit-text`, `edit-link`, `edit-media`, `block-add`
Allowed Navigation (data-linkable-allow)

p-11.plaintext: Add `data-linkable-allow` to elements that should navigate during edit mode (paging links, facet controls, etc.):

children.text: Add `data-linkable-allow` to elements that should navigate during edit mode (paging links, facet controls, etc.):
Path Syntax for Parent/Page Fields

p-14.plaintext: The `data-edit-text|edit-media|edit-link` attributes support Unix-style paths to edit fields outside the current block:

children.text: The `data-edit-text|edit-media|edit-link` attributes support Unix-style paths to edit fields outside the current block:

ul-15.plaintext: **`fieldName`** — edit the block's own field (default) **`../fieldName`** — edit the parent block's field **`../../fieldName`** — edit the grandparent's field **`/fieldName`** — edit the page metadata field

children.text: **`fieldName`** — edit the block's own field (default)
**`../fieldName`** — edit the parent block's field
**`../../fieldName`** — edit the grandparent's field
**`/fieldName`** — edit the page metadata field

Renderer Node-ID Rules

p-24.plaintext: When rendering Slate nodes to DOM, your renderer must follow these rules for `data-node-id`:

children.text: When rendering Slate nodes to DOM, your renderer must follow these rules for `data-node-id`:

ol-25.plaintext: Element nodes (`p`, `strong`, `em`, etc.) must have `data-node-id` matching the Slate node's `nodeId` Wrapper elements — If you add extra wrapper elements around a Slate node, ALL wrappers must have the same `data-node-id` as the inner element

children.text: Element nodes (`p`, `strong`, `em`, etc.) must have `data-node-id` matching the Slate node's `nodeId`
Wrapper elements — If you add extra wrapper elements around a Slate node, ALL wrappers must have the same `data-node-id` as the inner element

p-26.plaintext: hydra.js uses node-ids to map between Slate's data model and your DOM. When restoring cursor position after formatting changes, it walks your DOM counting Slate children.

children.text: hydra.js uses node-ids to map between Slate's data model and your DOM. When restoring cursor position after formatting changes, it walks your DOM counting Slate children.

tabs.label: Html
tabs.code: Valid wrapper pattern: bold Both elements have the same node-id, so they count as one Slate child. Invalid (missing node-id on wrapper): bold This breaks cursor positioning because hydra.js can't correlate DOM structure to Slate structure.
